feedback.text: In principle, a good first draft. I am delighted that something is being done. I also found it superseded by Dark patterns directly in the document, as I have been excited privately for a long time. However, one point I would like to point out is the point of licensing. Many manufacturers are going on and have their software or some hardware licensed instead of buying it. This has the negative effect that at the expiry of the licence (or if the manufacturer goes over, or no longer wants, etc.), this licence expires and the product is no longer usable. This is not only extremely unfriendly to suppliers, but also a waste of resources. Firms can no longer rely on the functioning of a software; Software manufacturers can simply let all licences be invented in case of shortages, except in addition to paying for them; Hardware, which could still be used well, suddenly becomes a very expensive brick; once purchased in a software, internal or external goods are suddenly worthless because the software is no longer working, etc. For example, manufacturers such as Broadcom (VMWare), Oracle, Adobe, etc., or many video games or professional administration software software (in the medical segment [as I know best] such as KIM or tomedo). ‘You will own nothing and be happy’ – let’s do something about it.
